editorial.headline: Rome: iconic landmarks, polished dining, and shopping that blends luxury fashion with timeless local style
editorial.intro: Rome is one of Europe’s strongest city-break destinations for travellers who want major landmarks, memorable food, and a sense of place that feels grand without needing an overly packed itinerary. It works best when the trip is planned by district, with time reserved for both major sights and slower neighbourhood browsing.
why_visit: A global destination for history, architecture, food, and classic city atmosphere
Strong fit for first-time Europe trips, couples, and premium short stays
Excellent shopping across luxury streets, iconic department stores, and local boutique districts
Neighbourhoods feel distinct and allow the stay to be shaped around style, pace, and priorities
highlights: Very good for 3 to 4 day city breaks
Strong fit for couples, first-time visitors, and shopping-led premium stays
Best results come from planning by district and avoiding too much cross-city movement in one day
transport_tips: Walk as much as possible within central areas, but allow more time than expected between districts.
Taxis are often the easiest option for dinner reservations, luxury shopping areas, and evening plans.
Book major attractions well in advance, especially the Colosseum and Vatican Museums.
Wear comfortable shoes, as Rome often involves long walks on cobbled streets and uneven surfaces.

neighborhood_tips.name: Historic Centre
neighborhood_tips.tip: Best for first-time visitors wanting classic Rome atmosphere and easy access to landmarks.

neighborhood_tips.name: Tridente
neighborhood_tips.tip: Ideal for luxury hotels, designer shopping, and a more polished premium city base.

neighborhood_tips.name: Trastevere
neighborhood_tips.tip: Best for local-feeling dining, evening energy, and a more characterful side of Rome.

neighborhood_tips.name: Monti
neighborhood_tips.tip: Good for boutique shopping, cafés, and a more stylish neighbourhood feel.

neighborhood_tips.name: Prati
neighborhood_tips.tip: A useful calmer base with refined streets and easier Vatican-side access.

restaurants.headline: Recommended restaurants in Rome
restaurants.description: A practical mix of classic Roman dining, polished premium options, and high-confidence guest recommendations.

content.short_description: One of Rome’s strongest classic Roman dining recommendations in a very central location.
content.long_description: Armando al Pantheon is one of the clearest Rome recommendations for guests who want proper Roman food with real credibility rather than a generic tourist meal. It works especially well for first-time visitors, food-led city breaks, and travellers wanting a dependable central lunch or dinner.
insider_tips: Book ahead, as it is frequently in demand.
A particularly strong choice when guests ask specifically for a classic Roman meal.

google_places.name: Armando al Pantheon
google_places.formatted_address: Salita de' Crescenzi, 31, 00186 Roma RM, Italy

recommendation.editor_note: One of the strongest classic Roman dining recommendations in Rome.

content.short_description: A high-confidence Rome dining recommendation for guests who care strongly about food quality.
content.long_description: Roscioli is one of the best-known and most dependable restaurant recommendations in Rome for travellers who want a meal with real food credibility. It works especially well for couples, repeat visitors, and guests building a food-led itinerary.
insider_tips: Advance booking is important.
A strong choice when guests want a more food-focused recommendation than a purely atmospheric one.

google_places.name: Roscioli
google_places.formatted_address: Via dei Giubbonari, 21, 00186 Roma RM, Italy

recommendation.editor_note: One of the strongest food-first restaurant recommendations in Rome.

content.short_description: A polished premium dining recommendation in central Rome with strong couples appeal.
content.long_description: Pierluigi is a strong choice for guests wanting a more refined dinner in Rome without moving into ultra-formal tasting-menu territory. It suits luxury travellers, celebratory trips, and polished city-break itineraries.
insider_tips: A good premium option when guests want a more elevated dinner than a casual trattoria.
Reserve well in advance for prime dinner times.

google_places.name: Pierluigi
google_places.formatted_address: Piazza de' Ricci, 144, 00186 Roma RM, Italy

recommendation.editor_note: A strong premium dinner recommendation for couples and polished Rome stays.

activities.headline: Top activities in Rome
activities.description: Major landmark and cultural recommendations that work especially well for high-quality short stays.

content.short_description: The clearest first-time Rome landmark recommendation and one of the city’s essential experiences.
content.long_description: The Colosseum is the strongest single activity recommendation in Rome for first-time visitors because it is iconic, memorable, and central to the city’s identity. It works especially well as a core anchor in a short stay itinerary.
insider_tips: Advance timed entry is essential.
Best combined with nearby ancient Rome sights rather than rushed as a quick standalone stop.

google_places.name: Colosseum
google_places.formatted_address: Piazza del Colosseo, 1, 00184 Roma RM, Italy

recommendation.editor_note: The core first-time activity recommendation in Rome.

content.short_description: One of Rome’s strongest cultural recommendations for guests wanting a major art and history experience.
content.long_description: The Vatican Museums are a high-confidence recommendation for guests who want one of Rome’s most important cultural visits. They are best suited to first-time visitors, art-focused travellers, and stays with enough time for a substantial museum experience.
insider_tips: Timed entry should be booked well in advance.
Best for guests who are comfortable dedicating real time and energy to a major museum visit.

recommendation.editor_note: A top cultural recommendation for guests wanting a major museum experience in Rome.

gyms.headline: Gyms and fitness options
gyms.description: High-confidence fitness recommendations for guests wanting more than standard hotel gym access.

content.short_description: The strongest practical gym recommendation for guests wanting serious fitness facilities in Rome.
content.long_description: Virgin Active clubs are the most reliable fitness recommendation pattern in Rome for guests who want full-service gym facilities rather than minimal hotel access. They suit longer stays, routine training, and travellers who want a mainstream premium fitness standard.
insider_tips: Best for guests who specifically ask for a proper gym, not just hotel fitness.
Match the branch to the guest’s hotel location before recommending.

external_ids.provider_note: Use nearest suitable Virgin Active branch based on guest location

recommendation.editor_note: The most practical serious-gym recommendation pattern in Rome.

content.short_description: A useful central recommendation for guests who prefer structured classes over open gym access.
content.long_description: A boutique fitness option in central Rome is a strong secondary recommendation for guests who prefer class-based workouts and a more modern studio format. It suits solo travellers, shorter stays, and guests who want convenience near the city core.
insider_tips: Best for guests who prefer structured sessions over general gym training.
A useful option when proximity to the historic centre matters.

recommendation.editor_note: A useful secondary option for guests who want central class-based fitness.

spa_wellness.headline: Spa and wellness recommendations
spa_wellness.description: High-quality wellness options for guests wanting a more restorative side of Rome.

content.short_description: One of Rome’s strongest premium spa recommendations for luxury and celebratory stays.
content.long_description: Amo Spa at Hassler Roma is a high-confidence choice for guests seeking a premium wellness experience in one of Rome’s most polished hotel settings. It suits couples, luxury travellers, and premium city-break itineraries built around the Spanish Steps area.
insider_tips: Best booked in advance for preferred treatment times.
A particularly strong recommendation for guests already staying near the Tridente and Spanish Steps area.

recommendation.editor_note: A top premium spa recommendation in central Rome.

content.short_description: A stronger resort-style wellness recommendation for guests wanting a fuller spa environment in Rome.
content.long_description: Rome Cavalieri Grand Spa Club is one of the better choices for guests who want a substantial luxury wellness environment rather than a smaller city spa. It suits slower leisure stays, premium wellness trips, and guests who value space and facilities.
insider_tips: Best for guests comfortable being outside the tight historic-core walking area.
A stronger fit for a wellness-focused half day than a quick city treatment stop.

recommendation.editor_note: A strong recommendation for guests wanting a fuller luxury wellness setting.

shopping.headline: Shopping recommendations in Rome
shopping.description: Rome shopping is a core part of the destination and works best when matched to guest style: luxury labels, iconic department stores, practical premium malls, boutique districts, and curated local speciality shopping.

content.short_description: Rome’s clearest luxury shopping recommendation for designer labels and polished premium browsing.
content.long_description: Via dei Condotti is the strongest shopping recommendation in Rome for guests seeking luxury fashion and a premium retail setting. It works especially well for luxury travellers, fashion-led stays, and guests based in the Tridente area.
highlights: Top luxury shopping street in Rome
insider_tips: Best for guests specifically asking for high-end designer shopping.
Works especially well when paired with premium dining or aperitivo nearby.

google_places.name: Via dei Condotti
google_places.formatted_address: Via dei Condotti, 00187 Roma RM, Italy

recommendation.editor_note: The top luxury shopping recommendation in Rome.

content.short_description: One of the most practical premium shopping recommendations in central Rome.
content.long_description: La Rinascente Roma Tritone is a very useful shopping recommendation for guests who want fashion, beauty, gifting, and convenience in one location. It is ideal for short stays where time is limited and a department-store format is more efficient than moving between multiple areas.
insider_tips: A good recommendation when guests want one reliable premium shopping stop.
Easy to combine with Via del Corso and the Tridente area.

google_places.name: La Rinascente Roma Tritone
google_places.formatted_address: Via del Tritone, 61, 00187 Roma RM, Italy

recommendation.editor_note: The strongest all-in-one premium shopping recommendation in central Rome.

content.short_description: A strong practical recommendation for guests wanting broad retail coverage in a mall-style format.
content.long_description: Euroma2 is the most useful Rome shopping-centre recommendation when guests want convenience, variety, and larger-scale retail rather than neighbourhood atmosphere or designer streets. It works best for families, longer stays, and practical shopping needs.
insider_tips: Not the most atmospheric Rome shopping choice, but very useful for convenience-led retail.
Best when guests want range and practicality in one place.

google_places.name: Euroma2
google_places.formatted_address: Viale dell'Oceano Pacifico, 83, 00144 Roma RM, Italy

recommendation.editor_note: The strongest mall-style shopping recommendation for practical retail needs in Rome.

content.short_description: Rome’s strongest boutique-shopping recommendation for local character and smaller labels.
content.long_description: Monti is one of the best shopping recommendations in Rome for guests who prefer independent boutiques, smaller lifestyle stores, and a more characterful neighbourhood atmosphere over formal luxury streets. It works especially well for couples, repeat visitors, and design-conscious travellers.
insider_tips: Best for slower browsing and neighbourhood atmosphere rather than a pure fashion mission.
Works well when paired with cafés and lunch rather than rushed shopping.

recommendation.editor_note: The best boutique-style shopping recommendation in Rome.

content.short_description: A high-quality Rome shopping recommendation for curated beauty, fragrance, and gifting.
content.long_description: Campomarzio70 is one of the strongest design-led shopping recommendations in Rome for guests wanting something more curated than general fashion retail. It suits premium travellers, gift shopping, and guests interested in fragrance and specialty luxury retail.
insider_tips: Best for guests interested in premium fragrance, beauty, and curated gifting.
Easy to include within a Tridente shopping day.

google_places.name: Campomarzio70
google_places.formatted_address: Via di Campo Marzio, 70, 00186 Roma RM, Italy

recommendation.editor_note: A standout curated-shopping recommendation in Rome.
